bead.title: bead: auto-commit attachment writes from execute-loop and migrate
bead.description:
PROBLEM
Two code paths write to .ddx/attachments/<bead-id>/events.jsonl as part of normal operation, and neither auto-commits the result. Operators end up with persistent untracked-and-modified worktree state that contradicts the project's intent (1083 attachment files ARE tracked across 11.6 MB of committed history — this is first-class audit-trail data, not detritus).

Concrete demonstration today:

1. Run `ddx try <id>` on 9 beads → 9 new untracked .ddx/attachments/<bead-id>/ dirs appear in main repo. Each contains routing decisions, cost-per-attempt, execution-routing-intent, prose-check.findings, execute-bead outcome — exactly the audit data the convention says we keep.
2. Run `ddx bead migrate` → 142 events externalized into existing tracked attachment files (showing as M), 1 new untracked attachment dir, 292 beads archived (modifying .ddx/beads.jsonl and .ddx/beads-archive.jsonl), and a fresh worktree of 151 dirty files. None committed.
3. The audit log clearly shows the historical pattern: commits like `878931ce3 chore(beads): commit missing event attachments` and `5d54258fd Merge bead ... attempt ... into main` periodically sweep up the accumulation — but it's recurring manual cleanup, not the steady-state design.

ROOT CAUSE
Two distinct writers, neither stages or commits its output:

A. The execute-loop emits per-attempt routing/cost/intent events. cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go emitRouteFailureEvent (line 3872) and the routing/cost emitters all call store.AppendEvent → the bead store writes to .ddx/attachments/<bead-id>/events.jsonl when the bead's events are externalized (or to the inline events array when not). The Store.AppendEvent path has no follow-up `git add`. The execute-loop's chore("add execution evidence") commit (visible in git log as `chore: add execution evidence [<run-id>]`) stages `.ddx/executions/<run-id>/` per AGENTS.md convention — but NOT `.ddx/attachments/<bead-id>/`.

B. cli/internal/bead/migrate.go ArchiveWithEvents (line 561) externalizes inline events from closed/archived beads to attachments. After externalizing 142 events today across many files, the migrate command exits without staging or committing the resulting changes. The operator command surface (cli/cmd/bead.go migrate handler) has no auto-commit step analogous to the bead store's other auto-commit paths.

PROPOSED FIX
1. Execute-loop landing path: extend the existing post-merge "chore(beads): commit missing event attachments"-style commit (or the bead's own evidence commit) to ALSO stage .ddx/attachments/<bead-id>/ for the bead just landed. Use the same git-index-lock recovery (cli/internal/gitlock per ddx-6a518fd9) so concurrent workers and the statusline poller do not race.
2. `ddx bead migrate` auto-commit: after a successful migrate that wrote any attachment changes (newly externalized + newly archived rows), call a single auto-commit at end-of-run with message `chore(beads): externalize and archive (<N> events to <M> attachments, <K> beads archived)`. Skip the commit entirely when no changes were written (idempotent re-run is a no-op).
3. Regression invariant: after `ddx try <id>` completes with `success`/`already_satisfied`, `git status --short` in the main repo MUST show zero untracked files under `.ddx/attachments/<bead-id>/` for that bead.
4. Operator escape hatch: `ddx bead reconcile-attachments` — single command that stages every untracked .ddx/attachments/ dir and any modified attachment files, commits as `chore(beads): reconcile missing attachment commits (<N> dirs, <M> modified)`. Useful for one-shot cleanup of pre-existing accumulation when this fix lands (today's 10 untracked + 141 modified dirs are an immediate use case).

NON-SCOPE
- Do not change WHAT gets externalized (the migrate event-externalization logic is fine).
- Do not change ATTACHMENT FORMAT or location.
- Do not add attachments to .gitignore (they ARE valuable cross-machine audit data per characterization).
- Do not auto-commit .ddx/executions/<run-id>/ — that's per-machine and stays untracked under existing convention.

DEPS
ddx-6a518fd9 (landed) — required so the auto-commits route through index-lock recovery and don't bounce on operator/statusline git contention.
bead.acceptance:
1. TestExecuteLoopLanding_StagesAttachmentDir asserts that after a `ddx try <id>` returns success, the .ddx/attachments/<id>/ dir for that bead is committed (`git ls-files .ddx/attachments/<id>/` returns the events.jsonl, and `git status --short` shows no untracked entries under that path).
2. TestExecuteLoopLanding_AttachmentsCommittedUnderIndexLockContention asserts the auto-commit succeeds even when a stale .git/index.lock is present at commit time (regression guard for the gitlock recovery integration).
3. TestBeadMigrate_AutoCommits asserts that `ddx bead migrate` against a repo with closed beads carrying inline events writes the attachments AND creates a single commit with message matching `^chore\(beads\): externalize and archive`. The commit's path list includes all modified attachment files and any new attachment dirs.
4. TestBeadMigrate_NoChangesNoCommit asserts `ddx bead migrate` against an already-migrated repo (no inline events to externalize, no beads to archive) does NOT create an empty commit.
5. TestBeadReconcileAttachments asserts the new `ddx bead reconcile-attachments` operator command stages and commits any untracked .ddx/attachments/ dirs and modified attachment files, and prints a summary line `chore(beads): reconcile missing attachment commits (<N> dirs, <M> modified)`.
6. TestBeadReconcileAttachments_NoChanges asserts the same command in a clean repo prints "no attachment changes to reconcile" and exits 0 without committing.
7. cd cli && go test ./cmd/... ./internal/bead/... ./internal/agent/... green.
8. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
